steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "emphasize the scope of Basquiat's work." Scope means the total size of his output — more than two thousand drawings and paintings. The correct choice must make that quantity the central point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "*Untitled* (*Pollo Frito*) is just one of more than two thousand drawings and paintings completed by American artist Jean-Michel Basquiat.": Places a single work in the context of his enormous total output ("just one of more than two thousand"), directly emphasizing scope. ✓
  • "Though artist Jean-Michel Basquiat completed most of his two thousand-plus drawings in the 1980s, his work *Untitled* (*Pollo Frito*) is composed of acrylic, oil, and enamel on canvas.": Mentions the quantity but shifts focus to the medium of a single work — a detail irrelevant to scope. ✗
  • "Decades after artist Jean-Michel Basquiat completed his 1982 work *Untitled* (*Pollo Frito*), a private collection purchased it for \$25.7 million.": Focuses on the sale price, not the breadth of his output. ✗
  • "At a 2018 auction, artist Jean-Michel Basquiat's *Untitled* (*Pollo Frito*), composed of acrylic, oil, and enamel on canvas, sold for \$25.7 million.": Focuses on auction details of a single work, not the overall scope. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
